name: Prefab Labor Camps
description:
Prefab Labor Camps — Factory-Prefabricated Camp Deployment for 12 to 18 Month Projects

Prefab labor camps ship every block of the worker-accommodation order from the factory floor — dormitory, sanitation, canteen, and supervisor quarter built and tested before the trucks roll out, assembled on site inside 10 days. SAMAN supplies prefab labor camps to highway, factory-expansion, power, and industrial projects across India, manufactured at our Bangalore and Greater Noida facilities for builds running 12 to 18 months.

What Tightens in the Order When the Spec Says "Prefab Labor Camp"

Three things change in the order the moment your BoQ line moves from "labor camp setup" to "prefab labor camp."

The first is block-set lock at PO. Every dormitory, sanitation block, canteen, and supervisor quarter is named at the line-item level with a dispatch date against each — not aggregated under one camp setup figure.

The second is per-unit consistency. Wall sealing, frame square, and electrical fitout hold to the same factory tolerance dormitory-to-dormitory, sanitation-to-canteen.

The third is fixed-date dispatch. Factory output schedule replaces on-site crew timing as the timeline driver.

Four Block Types Inside a Prefab Labor Camp Order — Dormitory, Sanitation, Canteen, Supervisor

A SAMAN prefab labor camp ships as four coordinated block types, each pre-finished at the factory.

Dormitory blocks — worker sleeping accommodation on insulated PUF sandwich panels over MS frames, with PPGI single-skin roofing. Bunk capacity matches your headcount.

Sanitation blocks — toilets and bathing points sized to BOCW Act ratios, with sealed plumbing ready for site hookup.

Canteen unit — cooking provisions, dining capacity, and ventilation openings fitted before line exit.

Supervisor quarter — separate sleeping plus a small project-office space for the camp supervisor.

Recreation infrastructure is not part of this format — that's the 18-plus-month prefab labour colony arrangement, covered separately.

A SAMAN prefab labor camp set up next to a factory-expansion warehouse build — workers commute from camp to construction zone within the same site fence line.

How the Four Blocks Are Produced in One Coordinated Factory Run

Each block type runs through parallel jig stations at the same SAMAN facility. MS frames are squared on jigs matched to block-type dimensions, PUF panels are cut to per-block sizes, and PPGI roofs are laid before any unit leaves the line. Every block is tested for frame square, panel-to-frame adhesion, electrical continuity, roof seal at overlap joints, and door-window alignment before it gets loaded on the dispatch trailer.

The 12 to 18 Month Project Window Where Prefab Camp Economics Work

Project duration is the cleanest filter for whether prefab camp economics pay back.

Under 12 months: prefab premium is hard to amortise — a standard labor camp built on site usually wins on cost.

12 to 18 months: prefab camp economics work — long enough to recover the factory-build premium through consistency and assembly-time savings, short enough that recreation fitout is overspec.

18 months and longer: configurations move up to a prefab labour colony with recreation space and washable long-stay finishes.

Worker-count band for the prefab camp is 80 to 400. Below 80, dispatch coordination is hard to amortise; at 400 plus, the configuration moves toward the colony tier.

An NHAI highway phase-job site uses a SAMAN prefab labor camp for the earthworks and paving crew — 14-month deployment, full camp redeployed to the next phase at project close.

Batched Dispatch and On-Site Assembly — From Truck Convoy to Camp-Ready in 10 Days

A prefab labor camp ships in 2 to 4 truck batches over 12 to 16 working days from confirmed PO. The first batch carries dormitory and sanitation blocks — these get assembled first so workers can move in by day 7. The canteen and supervisor quarter commission on the follow-up batch. Site assembly target for an 80 to 250 worker camp is 10 working days, assuming levelled ground and a clear truck-access route to the offload point.

A metro rail project camp during the second-batch unload — the canteen unit and supervisor quarter arrive after the dormitories and sanitation blocks are commissioned.

What the Prefab Premium Buys — Consistency, Dispatch Lock, Assembly Speed

A prefab labor camp carries a premium over a site-built equivalent. What the premium buys is specific: consistency across all four block types — same wall sealing, same frame tolerance, same electrical fitout dormitory after dormitory; dispatch lock at PO confirmation rather than open-ended on-site timelines; and assembly speed that gets a 200-worker camp commissioned in 10 days versus 20 to 30 working days for mixed-method builds.

A major bridge construction project uses a SAMAN prefab labor camp for its 300-worker site team — premium prefab format selected for dispatch certainty over the 16-month build.

How the Prefab Camp Quote Comes Together in 24 Hours

Five inputs let SAMAN quote a prefab labor camp inside 24 working hours:

  1. Worker count and shift pattern
  2. Project duration in months
  3. Land dimensions and ground condition at the proposed camp position
  4. Project state plus the nearest accessible road for trailer delivery
  5. Required site-readiness date

You receive a camp layout sketch with block positions, a full block-count breakdown, the batched dispatch schedule, and itemised pricing per block type.

A thermal power plant build uses a SAMAN prefab labor camp for 350 workers across a 17-month construction phase — the upper end of the prefab camp duration band.

Frequently Asked Questions

What does adding "prefab" to the labor camp specification actually change in the BoQ line items?

The line item moves from one aggregated "labor camp setup" figure to four named block lines — dormitory, sanitation, canteen, supervisor — each with quantity, dispatch date, and itemised price. The PO commits to factory output dates instead of on-site build estimates, and per-unit consistency becomes auditable line by line.

Does a SAMAN prefab labor camp include a worker recreation block?

No. The prefab labor camp format covers four block types — dormitory, sanitation, canteen, supervisor — without a recreation block. Recreation infrastructure is part of the prefab labour colony format for projects running 18 months and longer. If your project is 16 to 18 months and you want the recreation block, the quote routes to the colony spec.

When a prefab labor camp arrives in 2 to 4 truck batches, what gets assembled first?

Batch one carries dormitory and sanitation blocks, assembled in days 1 to 7 so workers can move in by the end of week one. Canteen and supervisor quarter ship on batch two and commission in days 8 to 10. Sequencing is locked at quote stage, not adjusted on site.

What's the typical price premium of a prefab labor camp over a site-built camp on a 200-worker project?

The premium varies with block mix and fitout level. Two things drive it: every block is factory-built rather than partly site-built, and the dispatch schedule carries a commitment cost. Buyers recover the premium through faster mobilisation, lower on-site rework, and asset value at redeployment.

After project closeout, can the full prefab camp be redeployed to the next site as one coordinated package?

Yes. A SAMAN prefab labor camp dismantles as one coordinated package — all four block types travel together to the next project. A 200-worker camp dismantles in 5 to 7 working days, loads onto the same truck convoy that delivered it, and reassembles on the original 10-day target.

Manufactured at our Bangalore and Greater Noida facilities.

Pan-India delivery in 12 to 16 working days from confirmed PO. For site accommodation under 50 workers, our prefabricated houses range covers bunkhouse units. Call or WhatsApp +91 97089 89937 to start a prefab camp quote, or browse the full labor colony range.
short_description:
FACTORY-PREFABRICATED LABOR CAMP
80 to 400 Workers · 12 to 18 Month Projects
Factory-prefabricated full camp deployment with four coordinated block types — dormitory, sanitation, canteen, supervisor quarter. Assembled on site in 10 working days.
SIZE / CAPACITY
80 to 400 workers
FRAME / PANEL
MS frame + PUF panels
PRICE / DELIVERY
On request / 12–16 days
FLOORLOAD / RELOCATIONS
Standard / 3 to 5 cycles
Bangalore + Greater Noida
+91 97089 89937
